name: Alba de Yeltes from Sancti-Spiritus — circular via Castraz
name_original: Alba de Yeltes desde Sancti-Spíritus — circular por Castraz
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ciudad Rodrigo traverse a diverse landscape, from the extensive pastures dotted with holm oaks to the rugged terrain of natural parks. The region features the Agueda River, offering routes along its plains, and significant elevation changes in areas like the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park. Further west, the Arribes del Duero region presents deep canyons carved by the Duero River. This variety provides a range of cycling experiences across different physical environments. Best touring cycling routes around Ciudad Rodrigo The most popular touring cycling route is Peña de Francia from Maíllo — circular by Monsago, a 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to Peña de Francia, a prominent peak in the Sierra de Francia. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Walled Cities Trail (GR-80), a difficult 68.6 miles (110.4 km) path. This route explores the historic defensive heritage of the region, including ancient walled cities. Local touring cyclists also love the El Bodón from Ciudad Rodrigo — circular by the río Agúeda, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail leading through the plains of the Agueda River, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Ciudad Rodrigo is defined by the plains of the Agueda River, the rugged terrain of the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park, and the canyons of the Arribes del Duero. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Ciudad Rodrigo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 161 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ciudad Rodrigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Ciudad Rodrigo, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists of all levels. These routes traverse varied landscapes, from river plains to rugged natural parks.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: Yes, Ciudad Rodrigo offers several easy touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047731093/alba-de-yeltes-from-sancti-spiritus-circular-via-castraz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alba de Yeltes from Sancti-Spiritus — circular via Castraz&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, covering 27.8 km with minimal elevation gain. In total, there are 3 easy routes to choose from.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The terrain around Ciudad Rodrigo is quite varied. You can expect routes along the plains of the Águeda River, offering gentler rides. However, many routes also venture into rugged areas like the &lt;strong&gt;Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Arribes del Duero&lt;/strong&gt; region, where you'll encounter significant elevation changes and deep canyons.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes, particularly those ascending to prominent peaks like Peña de Francia. A notable difficult route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319394266/pena-de-francia-from-maillo-circular-by-monsago" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Francia from Maíllo — circular by Monsago&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Overall, 14 of the routes are classified as difficult.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Ciudad Rodrigo pass by significant historical sites. Within the city, you can explore the medieval walls, the 14th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Enrique II of Trastámara&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3984587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor Ciudad Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319354463/walled-cities-trail-gr-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walled Cities Trail (GR-80)&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores the region's historic defensive heritage, including ancient walled cities and gates like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar Gate&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Ciudad Rodrigo are circular, making them convenient for day trips. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1320868505/el-bodon-from-ciudad-rodrigo-circular-by-the-rio-agueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bodón from Ciudad Rodrigo — circular by the río Agúeda&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the plains of the Agueda River, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1043044728/agallas-circular-from-ciudad-rodrigo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agallas — circular from Ciudad Rodrigo&lt;/a&gt; for a more challenging loop.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ciudad Rodrigo are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the serene river plains to the challenging climbs in the natural parks, and the opportunity to explore historic villages and natural beauty.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the natural parks near Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: Yes, the region is home to significant natural parks. Many routes traverse the &lt;strong&gt;Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its rugged terrain and iconic peaks like Peña de Francia. Further west, routes explore the dramatic canyons of the &lt;strong&gt;Arribes del Duero&lt;/strong&gt;, offering stunning views of the Duero River.

Q: What are some notable villages or towns to visit on a touring cycling trip?
A: The surrounding countryside is dotted with charming and historic villages perfect for a cycling stop. In the Sierra de Francia, you can visit &lt;strong&gt;La Alberca&lt;/strong&gt; (a national historic monument), &lt;strong&gt;Miranda del Castañar&lt;/strong&gt; with its castle, and &lt;strong&gt;Mogarraz&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its unique photographic tradition. Along the Arribes del Duero, villages like &lt;strong&gt;Trabanca&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;Villar del Buey&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;Fermoselle&lt;/strong&gt; offer insights into traditional architecture and local gastronomy.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The region around Ciudad Rodrigo is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially in the plains, while winter might bring cooler temperatures, particularly in the higher elevations of the natural parks. Checking local weather forecasts before your trip is always recommended.

Q: Are there routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Yes, many routes provide extensive panoramic views, especially those that climb to higher elevations. The challenging ascents to &lt;strong&gt;Peña de Francia&lt;/strong&gt; and the 'two ports' (Alto del Portillo and Puerto de Robledo) are particularly known for their breathtaking vistas across the landscape. Routes in the Arribes del Duero also offer dramatic views of the deep river canyons.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes typically take around Ciudad Rodrigo?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1320868505/el-bodon-from-ciudad-rodrigo-circular-by-the-rio-agueda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bodón from Ciudad Rodrigo — circular by the río Agúeda&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. More challenging and longer routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319354463/walled-cities-trail-gr-80" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walled Cities Trail (GR-80)&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 9 hours to complete.
tips: Sanctí-Spiritus is a town whose history goes back to the 12th century. It belongs to the region of the legendary Ciudad Rodrigo. It is one of the largest municipalities in the province, although the town has only 840 inhabitants. As a curiosity: there are several towns with the same name in the world, for example in Cuba and Argentina.
